question: Create a `Student` class with private attributes `studentID` (int), `name` (string), `major` (string). Implement public methods `setDetails(int id, string n, string m)` to initialize these attributes and `displayDetails()` to print them to the console.

test_cases.input: Student s1; s1.setDetails(101, "Alice Smith", "Computer Science"); s1.displayDetails(); Student s2; s2.setDetails(202, "Bob Junior", "Mechanical Engineering"); s2.displayDetails();
test_cases.expected:
Student ID: 101, Name: Alice Smith, Major: Computer Science
Student ID: 202, Name: Bob Junior, Major: Mechanical Engineering

errors: The `setDetails` method passes `std::string` parameters (`n_val`, `m_val`) by value, which incurs unnecessary string copy overhead. For efficiency, they should be passed by `const std::string&`.
The `displayDetails` method uses `std::endl`, which flushes the output buffer after each print. Using `\n` instead is generally more efficient when an explicit flush is not required.
Parameter names `i`, `n_val`, `m_val` in `setDetails` are less descriptive than standard practice, impacting code readability.
